name: Sonny Payne
abstract: Sonny Payne (May 4, 1926 – January 29, 1979) was an American jazz drummer, best known for his work with Count Basie and Harry James.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section was of course Freddie Green, Jo Jones, Eddie Jones and Sonny Payne and Count Basie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Radke
about_quotes.evidence: when I was with Harry and my dear old friend Sonny Payne, Sonny — we played really good together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Radke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Hillsdale, Michigan, November 8, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Porrello
about_quotes.evidence: On Harry James I had Buddy Rich for four years. Then when he left Sonny Payne — I mean Louie Bellson came on for a couple of months, then Sonny Payne came.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Porrello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jillean Williams
about_quotes.evidence: And Sonny Payne was the drummer... I was always very cognizant of the fact that a woman on the bus is like a bump on a log... that’s the band’s home
about_quotes.doc_title: Jillean Williams interviewed by Milt Fillius and Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 20, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: So eventually he got Sonny and Joe Williams came in at the same time you know. So that’s when, the band really started to get together you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Payne was playing drums with the band again... I said, 'Oh I’d love to.'... he kept me up there for the last ten tunes of the night.
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995
